2604.11753.title: Agentic Aggregation for Parallel Scaling of Long-Horizon Agentic Tasks
2604.11753.paper_summary: We study parallel test-time scaling for long-horizon agentic tasks such as agentic search and deep research, where multiple rollouts are generated in parallel and aggregated into a final response. While such scaling has proven effective for chain-of-thought reasoning, agentic tasks pose unique challenges: trajectories are long, multi-turn, and tool-augmented, and outputs are often open-ended. Aggregating only final answers discards rich information from trajectories, while concatenating all trajectories exceeds the model's context window. To address this, we propose AggAgent, an aggregation agent that treats parallel trajectories as an environment. We equip it with lightweight tools to inspect candidate solutions and search across trajectories, enabling it to navigate and synthesize information on demand. Across six benchmarks and three model families (GLM-4.7, Qwen3.5, MiniMax-M2.5), AggAgent outperforms all existing aggregation methods-by up to 5.3% absolute on average and 10.3% on two deep research tasks-while adding minimal overhead, as the aggregation cost remains bounded by a single agentic rollout. Our findings establish agentic aggregation as an effective and cost-efficient approach to parallel test-time scaling.
2604.11753.paper_summary_zh: 我們研究長期代理任務的平行測試時間擴展，例如代理搜索和深度研究，其中多個回合在平行中生成並聚合成最終回應。雖然這種擴展對於思考鏈推理已被證明是有效的，但代理任務提出了獨特的挑戰：軌跡長，回合多，並且增強了工具，輸出往往是開放式的。僅聚合最終答案會丟棄軌跡中的豐富信息，而將所有軌跡串聯起來則超出了模型的上下文窗口。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了AggAgent，一個將平行軌跡視為環境的聚合代理。我們為其配備了輕量級工具，以檢查候選解決方案並在軌跡中搜索，使其能夠按需導航和綜合信息。在六個基準和三個模型系列（GLM-4.7、Qwen3.5、MiniMax-M2.5）中，AggAgent在所有現有聚合方法中表現優於平均高達5.3%的絕對增益，以及在兩個深度研究任務中高達10.3%的增益，同時增加的開銷最小，因為聚合成本仍然受到單個代理回合的限制。我們的發現確立了代理聚合作為一種有效且具成本效益的平行測試時間擴展方法。
2604.11753.author: Yoonsang Lee et.al.
2604.11753.authors: Yoonsang Lee, Howard Yen, Xi Ye, Danqi Chen
